methods.description: This method involves measuring the magnetic properties of sediment samples using different instruments to understand their magnetic characteristics.

steps.description: Magnetic properties were measured using three types of dried subsamples: bulk sediment sealed in a non-magnetic plastic box, several tens of mg of bulk sediment sealed in a gelatin capsule and capped with glass wool, and several tens of mg of magnetic mineral extracts sealed in a gelatin capsule and capped with glass wool.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare subsamples for magnetic property measurement

steps.description: Each extraction of magnetic minerals was obtained using a hand magnet.
steps.searchable_summary: Extract magnetic minerals using a hand magnet

steps.description: The 'packed-in-plastic box' bulk subsamples were used in measurements with a MS2B magnetic susceptibility meter.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure magnetic susceptibility using MS2B meter

steps.description: Both of the bulk and extracts 'packed-in-gelatin capsule' subsamples were used in measurements with a MicroMag Model 3,900 vibrating sample magnetometer (VSM).
Calculate frequency-dependent susceptibility (k FD in %) using the formula: k FD = (k LFQ − k HFQ )/k LFQ × 100.
Prior to the VSM measurements, the 'packed-in-gelatin capsule' subsamples to be measured were weighed to permit calculation of mass-specific values.
steps.searchable_summary: Weigh subsamples for mass-specific value calculation

steps.description: Each hysteresis loop was measured up to maximum fields of ±0.5 T, and then, after correction for paramagnetic contribution within the loop, saturation magnetization (M s ), saturation remanence (M rs ), and magnetic coercivity (B c ) were calculated.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure hysteresis loop and calculate magnetic properties

steps.description: From each hysteresis loop, low-field magnetic susceptibility (χ lf) was calculated from the initial slope and high-field magnetic susceptibility (χ hf) was calculated from the high-field slope.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate low-field and high-field magnetic susceptibility

steps.description: From each progressive AF demagnetization of IRM, the remanence coercivity (B cr) was determined as the applied field at which the remanence becomes zero.
steps.searchable_summary: Determine remanence coercivity from AF demagnetization

steps.description: Each progressive IRM acquisition was made until 1 T was reached.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform IRM acquisition up to 1 T

steps.description: To unmix the magnetic mineral components contributing to the total remanent magnetization, each progressive IRM acquisition dataset was processed using the Max Unmix software.
steps.searchable_summary: Unmix magnetic mineral components using Max Unmix

steps.description: For each FORC analysis, 232 or 266 FORCs were measured; these FORC data were processed to create a FORC diagram using the FORCinel software.

methods.description: This method involves analyzing the mineralogy and granulometry of magnetic minerals using SEM observations and magnetic measurements.

steps.description: SEM observations provided back scattered electron images and EDS spectra of magnetic minerals in the sediments.
steps.searchable_summary: Obtain SEM images and EDS spectra of magnetic minerals

steps.description: From the observations, pyrite, greigite, and (titano)magnetite are identified.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify magnetic minerals from SEM observations

steps.description: The IRM component unmixing analyses revealed occurrence of magnetic mineral components with different coercivity spectra.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze IRM component unmixing for coercivity spectra

steps.description: Peters and Thompson discriminated between different magnetic minerals by constraining the typical ranges for the respective minerals in a biplot of IRM 1T /χ lf vs. B cr.
steps.searchable_summary: Discriminate magnetic minerals using biplot analysis

steps.description: Figure 10 presents FORC diagrams for four selective subsamples at different depths to provide additional information in terms of magnetic mineralogy and its domain state.
steps.searchable_summary: Visualize FORC diagrams for magnetic mineral analysis
